title: orthochilus clandestinus
intro: Orthochilus clandestinus, commonly known as the forest orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its multiple health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of Orthochilus clandestinus include its ability to modulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Orthochilus clandestinus can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high toxicity. In severe cases, it can cause cardiac arrest, seizures, and even death, especially when ingested in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can also lead to kidney and liver damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding and bruising. Misuse of this plant can also trigger allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, in some individuals.
